research.status_summary: Post-restructuring / cautiously selective hiring: Snap executed a major global layoff on April 15, 2026 — cutting ~1,000 employees (16% of full-time workforce) and closing 300+ open roles — with the TLV office retaining its AR/Lens Studio engineering function but showing near-zero open Israel-based roles on LinkedIn and Glassdoor as of June 2026.
research.hiring_policy: No Israel-specific sponsorship or EOR policy has been publicly updated post-layoffs; Glassdoor currently lists zero open Snap roles in Israel, and LinkedIn shows only 2 Snap Inc. postings in Israel — suggesting a hard hiring pause in-market while the global restructuring plays out through Q3 2026.
recent_news: April 15 2026: Snap announced ~1,000 global layoffs (16% of workforce) + closure of 300+ open roles, with $500M annualized cost savings target by H2 2026 (CNBC, Variety, Barchart).

CEO Evan Spiegel cited AI as a productivity lever: 'rapid advancements in artificial intelligence enable our teams to reduce repetitive work' — ~40% of new Snap code is already AI-generated per Q4 2025 earnings (InformationWeek).

Snap TLV office remains operational (Glassdoor profile active, office design intact) and Lens Studio's GenAI Suite (text-to-3D, GenAI Body Generator, Gaussian-splatting Head Gen) continued shipping updates through May 2026, pointing to preserved AR R&D investment in Israel despite headcount cuts.

Snap's 2026 Spectacles hardware release is still on roadmap per Lens Studio release notes, and Snap AR blog published fresh creator-reach content as recently as April 27, 2026, signaling continued platform investment.
research.growth_signal: Revenue growing ~10-12% YoY (~$1.72B in Q1 2026) and Snapchat MAU at 946M, but SNAP stock is down 26%+ YTD and the company is in a cost-reduction / AI-efficiency phase rather than a headcount-expansion phase, making net new Israel hiring unlikely before late 2026.
research.fit_for_lior: Strong long-term fit: Snap's TLV team sits at the exact intersection of Lior's Creative AI and AR/Lens Studio threads — the GenAI Suite (text-to-Lens, no-code AR creation) is a direct analogue to vibe-coding, and a Product Analyst or Creator-tools PM role would map tightly to her profile — but the current post-layoff hiring freeze means timing is poor until Q4 2026 at earliest.
